artifact.content_preview:
From: j.henderson@techsupplier.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:14:32 -0500

Alice,

Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation
with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre
migration.

Unfortunately, our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse
management system failure that has affected ou

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches, pushing arrival from March 6 to March 20, 2026 — after the March 8 migration window opens, making it impossible to complete staging and testing before the window begins.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a critical supply chain vulnerability with no readily available equivalent alternative routing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Alternative routing via Vancouver depot would extend the delay by an additional 3–5 business days beyond March 20, potentially pushing delivery to approximately March 25–27, further compressing or eliminating any remaining schedule buffer.

evidence_refs.excerpt: alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre may not be fully resolved, creating risk that the March 20 revised delivery date is itself optimistic and subject to further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Insufficient staging and testing time: even if equipment arrives March 20, there is no time allocated for pre-migration staging and testing that was explicitly required to be completed before the March 8 migration window, risking untested equipment being deployed into production.
affected_stakeholders: SSC end users dependent on migrated services

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Q2 migration timeline for NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration is at risk of being missed entirely, which may trigger contractual penalties, government reporting obligations, or downstream program dependencies tied to Q2 delivery commitments.
affected_stakeholders: Treasury Board or other oversight bodies

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Potential budget impact from expedited shipping costs, emergency procurement of alternative equipment, or costs associated with rescheduling migration activities (e.g., contractor overtime, extended facility bookings).

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our team is working around the clock to expedite the shipment.

risks.description: Resource scheduling risk: technical staff, contractors, and data centre personnel mobilized for the March 8 migration window may need to be stood down and rescheduled, risking resource unavailability for a rescheduled window.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Compliance risk: as a Government of Canada (SSC) project, NGIS Phase 3 may be subject to Treasury Board project management policy, PSPC supply arrangements, or contractual SLAs that impose obligations when delivery milestones are missed, and the delay may trigger mandatory reporting or change control processes.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Implicit communication and governance risk: the delay was first discussed verbally on a Friday call and only confirmed in writing the following Monday, suggesting a gap in formal change notification procedures that could delay escalation and decision-making.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration.

risks.description: Hidden dependency risk: the email reveals no contingency or fallback plan exists for equipment non-delivery, suggesting the project has no documented risk mitigation strategy for supply chain disruption on critical path hardware.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Please let me know how we can best support your planning activities during this disruption.

risks.description: Vendor reliability risk: TechSupplier's WMS failure and single-source supply model expose SSC to ongoing supplier concentration risk for future phases or procurements, with no indication of a secondary qualified vendor.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse.
